findings.title: Cache heap corruption confirmed but signature verification blocks descriptor reuse
findings.statement: Oversized manifest entries overflow the cache buffer and corrupt an adjacent cache_entry struct. GDB breakpoints confirm the size field corruption enables controlled re-allocation. The heap-analysis skill mapped the cache allocator layout. However, the descriptor-reuse stage requires corrupting the update package fd, which is gated by a signature verification step using a pinned certificate. The exploit-pivot skill evaluated data-only alternatives but the signature check is on the critical path — the fd is not accessible until after verification passes.

evidence.summary: debug_gdb_commands: breakpoints on cache write path confirm heap overflow and adjacent cache_entry size corruption
decomp_ghidra_decompile: signature verification on pinned cert gates fd access; no bypass path found
